# Weapons (Primary, Secondary, Melee, Arch-Guns, Arch-Melee)
FORMAT: Each ## entry is one weapon. Meta line: Type | Mastery Req | Riven Disposition. Each ### is an attack mode with **Damage:** breakdown and inline stat line (Crit Chance | Crit Multiplier | Status Chance | Fire Rate). ### Crafting has component tables.
INDEX: Primary Weapons (217 entries) | Secondary Weapons (146 entries) | Melee Weapons (258 entries) | Arch-Guns (20 entries) | Arch-Melee Weapons (8 entries)

# Primary Weapons
All primary weapons including rifles, shotguns, bows, and launchers. Includes damage types, critical stats, status chance, and crafting info.

## Acceltra
Using a barrage of rapid-fire plasma rockets, Gauss' signature weapon lays down a path of destruction. Reloads are faster while sprinting, even more so in Gauss' hands. For safety, rockets arm after traveling a safe distance.

### Wiki
Weapons
Using a barrage of rapid-fire plasma rockets, Gauss' signature weapon lays down a path of destruction. Reloads are faster while sprinting, even more so in Gauss' hands. For safety, rockets arm after traveling a safe distance.
The Acceltra, a rapid-fire micro-missile launcher. The smooth polycarbonate barrels still carry a vague stench of ozone.
Some think Gauss was a blunt instrument, all speed, with as much versatility as a cannonball. But the Acceltra implies more. It implies surging in, inviting the enemy to consider the blade, then rebounding to let missiles answer their confusion.
The Acceltra is Gauss's signature automatic micro-missile launcher with high critical chance, critical multiplier, fire rate, and explosive splash damage. The rockets arm after traveling a set distance to protect the user, and the launcher reloads faster while sprinting. When wielded by Gauss, the sprint reload boost is doubled.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Puncture and Impact damage.
Rockets have a minimum arming distance of 7 meters and explode in a 4 meter radius after impacting a surface or enemy.
Initial hit and explosion apply status separately.
Explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
Can benefit from Firestorm (Primed Firestorm) and Cautious Shot.
Rocket direct impacts have a guaranteed Impact proc.
Passive: Reloading while sprinting gives a +25% reload speed bonus (+50% when used by Gauss).
Minimum arming distance reduces the potential of self-stagger unless rapidly moving to the rocket's impact before exploding.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 8 | **Riven Disposition:** 1

### Rocket Impact
**Damage:** impact: 35
Crit Chance: 32.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2.8x | Status Chance: 6.00% | Fire Rate: 12

### Rocket Explosion
**Damage:** slash: 8.8, puncture: 35.2
Crit Chance: 32.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2.8x | Status Chance: 6.00% | Fire Rate: 12

## Acceltra Prime
Engage your enemies with deadly speed. This weapon reloads even faster when its wielder sprints, faster still with Gauss.

### Wiki
Weapon
Engage your enemies with deadly speed. This weapon reloads faster when its wielder sprints, faster still with Gauss
Acceltra Prime is the Primed counterpart of the Acceltra micro-missile launcher, sporting increased damage, Critical Chance, Critical Multiplier, Status Chance, explosion radius, and Reload Speed, at the expense of some Fire Rate. Acceltra Prime was released alongside Gauss Prime and Akarius Prime.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Puncture and Impact damage.
Rockets have a minimum arming distance of 7 meters and explode in a 5 meter radius after impacting a surface or enemy.
Initial hit and explosion apply status separately.
Explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
Can benefit from Firestorm (Primed Firestorm) and Cautious Shot.
Rocket direct impacts have a guaranteed Impact proc.
Passive: Reloading while sprinting gives a +25% reload speed bonus (+50% when used by Gauss/Gauss Prime).
Acceltra
**Notes:**
While sprinting, reload speed is increased by 25%, or 50% when used by Gauss/Gauss Prime. Without other mods, sprinting reload speed is 1.4 seconds, or 1.2 seconds with Gauss.
The Acceltra Prime's rockets have a minimum arming distance of 7 meters, meaning that they will not explode if they make contact with objects or enemies within that range, and thus making the Acceltra Prime safe to use in close quarters combat.
Arming distance is unaffected by Projectile Speed.
Regardless, it's still possible to deal self-stagger if the wielder is moving fast enough (either through Maneuvers and/or using certain Abilities) to catch up to the rockets.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 14 | **Riven Disposition:** 1

### Rocket Impact
**Damage:** impact: 44
Crit Chance: 34.00% | Crit Multiplier: 3x | Status Chance: 18.00% | Fire Rate: 10

### Rocket Explosion
**Damage:** slash: 10.6, puncture: 42.4
Crit Chance: 34.00% | Crit Multiplier: 3x | Status Chance: 18.00% | Fire Rate: 10

| Orokin Cell | 10 | — |

## Aeolak
This unusual automatic rifle feels strangely familiar and has two fire modes. Primary fire packs radiation damage. Alternate fire charges up to launch an explosive projectile.

### Wiki
This unusual automatic rifle feels strangely familiar and has two fire modes. Primary fire packs radiation damage. Alternate fire charges up to launch an explosive projectile.
Aeolak is a rifle repurposed by the Void's Thrax Legates. Its primary fire shoots highly accurate fully-automatic rounds while its Alternate Fire doubles as a grenade launcher.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Puncture damage on its primary fire and Blast damage on its Alternate Fire.
Alternate Fire charges a powerful round that explodes.
Shots explode in a 7 meter radius on impact with a surface or enemy.
Initial hit and explosion apply status separately.
Explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
Can benefit from Firestorm (Primed Firestorm) and Cautious Shot.
Charge time is not affected by fire rate mods.
Projectile is unaffected by Punch Through.
Charge is automatic and does not need to be held down.
Unless held down, the weapon fires as soon as it has reached a full charge, which can lead to premature firing.
Consumes 10 ammo per shot.
Alternate Fire:
Explosion inflicts self-stagger.
Explosion has a headshot multiplier of 1x and cannot trigger headshot conditions.
Explosion has linear Damage Falloff from 100% to 50% from central impact.
Aeolak's main blueprint can be acquired from Tier 5 Chrysalith Bounties. The barrel blueprint drops from Void Cascade. The receiver and stock blueprints drop from Void Flood. All components can be traded, but not the main blueprint.
**Notes:**
The Aeolak's ability to act both as an assault rifle and as a grenade launcher makes the gun somewhat functionally similar to the Corpus Tenet Tetra and Stahlta, and the Entrati Trumna.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 10 | **Riven Disposition:** 4

### Normal Attack
**Damage:** impact: 15, slash: 17, puncture: 23, radiation: 5
Crit Chance: 21.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2.3x | Status Chance: 33.00% | Fire Rate: 6

### Alt-Fire
**Damage:** impact: 58.2, slash: 29.1, puncture: 9.7
Crit Chance: 21.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2.3x | Status Chance: 33.00% | Fire Rate: 1.5

### Alt-Fire Explosion
**Damage:** blast: 789
Crit Chance: 21.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2.3x | Status Chance: 33.00% | Fire Rate: 1.5

### Crafting
| Component | Count | Drops |
|---|---|---|
| Barrel | 1 | Zariman/Tuvul Commons (Void Cascade), Rotation C (10.00%) |

| Receiver | 1 | Zariman/Everview Arc (Void Flood), Rotation C (10.00%) |

## Afentis
Styanax's speargun matches his might. Throw Afentis to pin an enemy and nearby enemies will also be stunned. Throw Afentis onto the ground to buff nearby allies with Ballistarii Might. The buff increases reload speed, fire rate, and ammo pools and reduces recoil. Allies who kill enemies with Ballistarii Might maintain the buff temporarily when they move away from Afentis.

### Wiki
Weapons
Styanax's speargun matches his might. Throw Afentis to pin an enemy and nearby enemies will also be stunned. Throw Afentis onto the ground to buff allies with Ballistarii Might. The buff increases reload speed, fire rate, and ammo pools and reduces recoil. Allies who kill enemies with Ballistarii Might maintain the buff temporarily when they move away from Afentis.
As Styanax struck and struck again with the Afentis, making little headway against the relentless Leviathan, a final desperate scream from Aria achieved the impossible.
It awoke Darro.
From out of those thousands of fused bodies, a single mind was suddenly saying no.
Latching his remaining arms around his tormentor's throat with the very last of his life's strength, the doomed Darro distracted the Philanthropist long enough for Styanax to act.
One last act of love and protection, for his sister.
But the Warframe hesitated. It seems he understood the importance of Darro's rebellion.
If one mind could turn upon its master... why not all of them?
With but a gesture, he gave the captive minds their freedom.
Suddenly awakened and aware of their hellish plight, the damned townspeople tore at the Philanthropist's exposed face, the Leviathan's body coming apart as arms broke free from the mass, clawing for revenge.
Together they dragged the screaming Philanthropist down, rending and tearing.
In that moment, we can be assured the Philanthropist knew the depth of his failure.
His defeat.
Without worshipers, a god... is nothing.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 8 | **Riven Disposition:** 3

### Semi

## Akaten
Hound melee attacks eviscerate with Puncture and Slash Damage.

### Acquisition
The Akaten is automatically acquired upon claiming a Hound configured with the Hec Model. Note that this weapon also takes up one Sentinel inventory slot.

### Wiki
Hound melee attacks eviscerate with Puncture and Slash damage.
Akaten is a Hound weapon that eviscerates enemies with Puncture and Slash damage.
This weapon can be sold for 5,000.
Characteristics
This weapon inflicts Puncture and Slash.
Advantages:
Very high critical multiplier.
Good status chance.
Disadvantages:
Low critical chance.
**Notes:**
The Akaten, Batoten and Lacerten are all statistically identical (speed, critical, status, etc.) other than their physical damage types. All three are 50%/50% split between two physical damages, with the three weapons covering the three possible combinations.
Akaten only uses standard melee attacks. It does not allow the Hound to block or perform special attacks, nor does it have its own combo counter.
As such, Akaten cannot equip mods that affect heavy attacks (e.g. Killing Blow), slide attacks (e.g. Maiming Strike), combo counter (e.g. Blood Rush), finishers (e.g. Finishing Touch), or blocking (e.g. Focused Defense). It also cannot equip any Set Mods (e.g. Gladiator Might).
Range mods like Reach do increase the hounds melee range. Their base attack range is very low and because of that they tend to miss many of their attacks. Equipping a range mod makes their attacks hit more consistently.
Heavy attack windup speed (through Melee Elementalist) increases the hounds attack speed.
Actual attack speed mods have no effect.
**Type:** Companion Weapon | **Riven Disposition:** 3

### Normal Attack
**Damage:** slash: 150, puncture: 150
Crit Chance: 10.00% | Crit Multiplier: 3x | Status Chance: 15.00% | Fire Rate: 1

## Alternox
Gyre's signature weapon has two fire modes. Primary fire shocks enemies with electrical orbs. Alternate fire is a large ball of electricity that sticks to any surface and pulses Electricity before it explodes. In Gyre's hands, the weapon has a small amount of multishot.

### Wiki
Tenno
Gyre's signature weapon has two fire modes. Primary fire shocks enemies with electrical orbs. Alternate fire is a large ball of electricity that sticks to any surface and pulses Electricity before it explodes. In Gyre's hands, this weapon has a small amount of multishot.
The Alternox is Gyre's signature rifle cannon. The primary fire lobs Electricity orbs, while the Alternate Fire releases a sphere that acts like a smaller-scale Arcsphere, emitting pulses that deal damage to nearby enemies for ten seconds before exploding. Receives Multishot in the hands of Gyre.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Electricity Damage.
Primary fire shoots slow but fairly wide projectiles, larger than visually displayed.
Alternate Fire shoots a sphere that sticks to surfaces and enemies, then pulses once per second over 10 seconds before exploding. Each pulse has a range of 6 meters.
Can only have one deployed sphere at a time. Pressing Alternate Fire again will immediately detonate the sphere.
Initial hit and explosion apply status separately.
Pulses and explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
Can benefit from Firestorm (Primed Firestorm) and Cautious Shot.
Projectile has heavy arcing.
Consumes 4 ammo per shot.
When wielded by Gyre, this weapon gains a 20% multishot increase, additive with other multishot mods.
Primary fire:
Projectiles have a thickness of ? meters.
Innate Infinite Body Punch Through.
All firing modes have a headshot multiplier of 1x.
Alternate Fire:
Not affected by multishot.
Explosion cannot trigger headshot conditions.
Explosion inflicts self-stagger, however the pulses do not.
Pulses and explosion have linear Damage Falloff from 100% to 40% from central impact.
Alternox Prime
Alternox's blueprint can be acquired from Tier 3 Chrysalith Bounties.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 8 | **Riven Disposition:** 4

### Normal Attack
**Damage:** electricity: 62
Crit Chance: 14.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2.2x | Status Chance: 30.00% | Fire Rate: 4.33

### Alt-Fire Contact
**Damage:** impact: 5, slash: 7.5, puncture: 37.5
Crit Chance: 2.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2x | Status Chance: 44.00% | Fire Rate: 1.5

### Alt-Fire Damage over Time
**Damage:** electricity: 50
Crit Chance: 2.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2x | Status Chance: 44.00% | Fire Rate: 1

### Alt-Fire Explosion
**Damage:** electricity: 100
Crit Chance: 2.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2x | Status Chance: 44.00% | Fire Rate: 1

Orokin gold enhances the conductivity of Gyre Prime's signature rifle, enabling it to fire massive electrifying orbs.

Weapon
Orokin gold enhances the conductivity of Gyre Prime's signature rifle, enabling it to fire massive electrifying orbs.
Alternox Prime is the Primed counterpart of the Alternox pulse cannon, sporting enhanced damage, primary fire critical chance and fire rate, status chance, and magazine size. Alternox Prime was released alongside Gyre Prime and Kestrel Prime.
Characteristics

Primary fire shoots slow but fairly wide projectiles, larger than visually displayed.
Alternate Fire shoots a sphere that sticks to surfaces and enemies, then pulses once per second over 10 seconds before exploding. Each pulse has a range of 6 meters.
Can only have one deployed sphere at a time. Pressing Alternate Fire again will immediately detonate the sphere.
Initial hit and explosion apply status separately.
Pulses and explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
Can benefit from Firestorm (Primed Firestorm) and Cautious Shot.
Projectile has heavy arcing.
Consumes 4 ammo per shot.
When wielded by Gyre, this weapon gains a 20% multishot increase, additive with other multishot mods.
Innate two Madurai and one Naramon polarities.
Primary fire:
Projectiles have a thickness of ? meters.
Innate Infinite Body Punch Through.
All firing modes have a headshot multiplier of 1x.
Alternate Fire:
Not affected by multishot.
Explosion cannot trigger headshot conditions.
Explosion inflicts self-stagger, however the pulses do not.
Pulses and explosion have linear Damage Falloff from 100% to 40% from central impact.
Alternox

When equipped by Gyre/Gyre Prime, the weapon gains 20% Multishot.

## Ambassador
Bring negotiations to an instant conclusion with this Corpus assault rifle. Switch effortlessly between charged explosive shots and rapid-fire electrical assault mode.

### Wiki
Weapons
Bring negotiations to an instant conclusion with this Corpus assault rifle. Switch effortlessly between charged explosive sniper shots and rapid fire electrical assault mode
The Ambassador is a Corpus assault rifle whose Alternate Fire allows switching between fully-automatic rounds or a long-ranged explosive shot.
Characteristics
This weapon primarily deals Electricity damage.
Alternate Fire mode-switching reloads the weapon and changes the weapon's firing mode to a charged shot.
Charge mode:
Pinpoint accuracy.
Heavy Caliber's accuracy penalty is minimal.
Shots explode in a 6 meter radius on impact with a surface or enemy.
Initial hit and explosion apply status separately.
Explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
Can benefit from Firestorm (Primed Firestorm) and Cautious Shot.
Charge is automatic and does not need to be held down.
However, holding down the trigger will not fire the shot at full charge thus allowing for aim adjustments.
Rolling while holding the full charge will cancel it and not consume any ammo.
Consumes 16 ammo per shot, effectively resulting in a 6-round magazine.
Charge mode:
Explosion inflicts self-stagger.
Explosion has a headshot multiplier of 1x and cannot trigger headshot conditions.
Explosion has linear Damage Falloff from 100% to 50% from central impact.
Alternate Fire mode-switching is not instantaneous and has an animation equal to the reload speed.
This weapon's main blueprint can be acquired from Rotation C of Empyrean Survival missions in Venus Proxima, Neptune Proxima, Pluto Proxima, and Veil Proxima. The component blueprints are dropped by Aurax Atloc Raknoids and Narmer Raknoids. All parts can be traded between players.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 10 | **Riven Disposition:** 4

### Auto
**Damage:** electricity: 29
Crit Chance: 14.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2.8x | Status Chance: 26.00% | Fire Rate: 13.33

### Charge
**Damage:** electricity: 600
Crit Chance: 16.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2.8x | Status Chance: 32.00% | Fire Rate: 1

### Charged AoE
**Damage:** electricity: 800
Crit Chance: 16.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2.8x | Status Chance: 32.00% | Fire Rate: 1

### Crafting
| Component | Count | Drops |
|---|---|---|
| Barrel | 1 | Aurax Atloc Raknoid (5.00%); Narmer Raknoid (5.00%) |
| Blueprint | 1 | Venus/Luckless Expanse (Skirmish), Rotation C (12.50%); Veil Proxima/Lu-Yan (Skirmish), Rotation C (16.67%); Neptune/Enkidu Ice Drifts (Skirmish), Rotation C (8.33%); Pluto/Fenton's Field (Skirmish), Rotation C (8.33%) |
| Receiver | 1 | Aurax Atloc Raknoid (5.00%); Narmer Raknoid (5.00%) |

## Amprex
The Amprex rifle fires a continuous beam of high voltage electricity that arcs among nearby enemies. This weapon excels at Crowd Control.

### Acquisition
The Amprex's blueprint can be researched from the Energy Lab in the dojo.
This weapon can be sold for 7,500.

### Wiki
Weapons
The Amprex rifle fires a continuous beam of high voltage electricity that arcs among nearby enemies. This weapon excels at crowd control.
The Amprex is a Corpus beam rifle that fires a continuous stream of Electricity arcs that can chain between units, making it a devastating crowd control weapon.
Characteristics
This weapon deals Electricity damage.
Heavy Caliber's accuracy penalty is minimal on this weapon.
Beam can chain up to 3 nearby enemies within 10 meters of each other, with each chain doing 50% of the previous chain's damage.
Each enemy hit by the main beam from Punch Through can generate a new set of 3 chains.
Enemy corpses will still allow the beam to connect to nearby enemies.
Second highest critical chance of all continuous and automatic primaries, behind Synapse.
Consumes 0.5 ammo per tick of damage.
Damage ramps up from 30% to 100% over 0.6 seconds when firing. After firing stops for 0.8 seconds, the damage decays back to 30% over 2 seconds.
Main beam has a limited range of 18 meters.
Sinister Reach can be equipped to extend the maximum range.
Chain beam has a limited range of 10 meters.
Chain beam range cannot be increased with Sinister Reach.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 10 | **Riven Disposition:** 2

### Normal Attack
**Damage:** electricity: 22
Crit Chance: 32.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2.2x | Status Chance: 22.00% | Fire Rate: 12

### Crafting
| Component | Count | Drops |
|---|---|---|
| Argon Crystal | 3 | — |
| Blueprint | 1 | — |
| Ferrite | 9000 | — |
| Fieldron | 8 | — |
| Plastids | 600 | — |

## Arca Plasmor
Stagger targets with blasts from this Corpus engineered plasma shotgun. Surviving enemies are consumed with radiation.

### Acquisition
The Arca Plasmor's blueprint can be researched from the Energy Lab in the dojo.

### Wiki
Weapons
Stagger targets with blasts from this Corpus engineered plasma shotgun. Surviving enemies are consumed with radiation.
The Arca Plasmor is a Corpus-built plasma shotgun that fires highly damaging pulses of Radiation with a guaranteed Impact proc. The projectiles it fires are wide and have infinite Punch Through on bodies, making it highly effective in mowing down large groups of enemies, but suffers from a low critical multiplier and no bonus damage on headshots.
Characteristics
This weapon deals high amounts of Radiation damage.
Shots have a guaranteed Impact proc to enemies up to 14 meters.
Distance is affected by Projectile Speed.
Killed enemies will have their corpses vaporized, good for Stealth gameplay if silenced with Silent Battery due to no bodies being left behind.
Enemies defeated with this weapon disintegrate quickly, making it ill-suited to use alongside Nekros' Desecrate.
Two innate Madurai polarities.
Low zoom, making covering wide shots while aiming easier.
Projectiles have a thickness of 3 meters.
Innate Infinite Body Punch Through.
Very ammo efficient.
Has linear damage falloff from 100% to 33.33% from 10m to 20m target distance (distances are affected by Projectile Speed).
Projectile has travel time.
Limited range of 30 meters (can be increased with Projectile Speed mods).
Has a headshot multiplier of 1x.
Innate punch through does not apply to surfaces if hitting with the central portion of the projectile, requiring punch through mods.
Tenet Arca Plasmor
**Type:** Shotgun | **Mastery Req:** 10 | **Riven Disposition:** 3

## Argonak
Pick off targets by highlighting distant enemies using this Grineer rifle's advanced laser sighting system. Deadly in both single fire and automatic modes.

### Acquisition
The Argonak's blueprint can be researched from the Chem Lab in the dojo.

### Wiki
Grineer
Pick off targets by highlighting distant enemies using this Grineer rifle's advanced laser sighting system. Deadly in both single fire and automatic modes.
The Argonak is a Grineer assault rifle that highlights enemies close to the reticle when aiming as long as they are within range, increasing visibility in dark areas. Its semi-auto main fire can be quickly changed to its secondary automatic mode to engage enemies with a faster fire rate and increased status chance at the cost of lowering critical chance.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Slash damage.
When aiming, highlights enemies within a 100m range using weapon's energy color, increasing visibility.
Can use the Argonak-exclusive Amalgam Argonak Metal Auger mod.
Innate Madurai and one Naramon polarities.
Semi-auto mode has high zoom, increasing its effectiveness at longer ranges.
Semi-auto mode's high zoom impairs close quarter combat capability.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 7 | **Riven Disposition:** 4

### Semi-Auto Mode
**Damage:** impact: 24.51, slash: 26.22, puncture: 6.27
Crit Chance: 27.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2.3x | Status Chance: 19.00% | Fire Rate: 4.33

## Artax
Freeze targets in their tracks with this sentinel mounted ice-beam.

### Acquisition
The Artax is automatically acquired upon obtaining Taxon. Note that this weapon also takes up one Companion inventory slot.

### Wiki
Freeze targets in their tracks with this sentinel mounted ice-beam.
The Artax is the default robotic weapon of the Taxon Sentinel, which fires a beam that deals Cold damage and status effect against enemies. This weapon accepts Rifle Mods.
Characteristics
This weapon deals Cold damage.
**Notes:**
Has an innate +20% Cold damage bonus.
Does not combine with other modded elements, and will stay as Cold.
**Trivia:**
The Artax is possibly named as a portmanteau of "Arctic" and "Taxon."
Back when Riven Mods for Robotic Weapons could be obtained via Sorties, Artax Rivens were only available from Update 19.10 to Update 19.10.1, a five-day period where players could potentially get them. Thus, Artax Rivens were the rarest Riven Mods in circulation, until Update 25.7 made Robotic Weapon Rivens exclusively available from Cephalon Simaris.
**Type:** Companion Weapon | **Riven Disposition:** 3

### Normal Attack
**Damage:** cold: 5
Crit Chance: 2.00% | Crit Multiplier: 1.5x | Status Chance: 35.00% | Fire Rate: 16.67

## Astilla
Blast enemies with glass slugs that devastate on impact. In Gara's hands, this signature weapon has a larger Ammo Pool.

### Acquisition
The Astilla's blueprint can be purchased from the Market.

### Wiki
Weapons
Blast enemies with glass slugs that devastate on impact. In Gara's hands, this signature weapon has a larger Ammo Pool.
The Astilla is Gara's signature shotgun, firing glass slugs that explode and damage nearby enemies in a small radius. With strong Impact and Slash damage, this weapon stuns and bleeds enemies with ease. When in Gara's hands, the shotgun holds more Ammo Maximum.
This weapon can be sold for 7,500.
Characteristics
This weapon primarily deals Slash damage.
Fires a single slug every shot instead of individual pellets.
Slugs have a guaranteed Impact proc.
Slugs explode in a 2.4 meter radius after impacting a surface or enemy.
Initial hit and explosion apply status separately.
Explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
Innate Madurai and Naramon polarities.
Tied with Astilla Prime for the highest accuracy of all shotguns.
Has linear damage falloff from 100% to 50% from 40m to 60m target distance (distances are affected by Projectile Speed).
Fewer status counts due to firing a single projectile instead of multiple pellets.
Projectiles have travel time.
Shatter area of effect cannot hit enemies behind the location of impact.
Explosion inflicts self-stagger.
Explosion has a headshot multiplier of 1x and cannot trigger headshot conditions.
Explosion has linear Damage Falloff from 100% to 70% from central impact.
Astilla Prime
**Type:** Shotgun | **Mastery Req:** 10 | **Riven Disposition:** 4

## Astilla Prime
Loose explosive glass slugs that tear through enemies with Gara's signature shotgun, presented here in its spectacular Prime form.

### Wiki
Loose explosive glass slugs that tear through enemies with Gara's signature shotgun, presented here in its spectacular Prime form.
Astilla Prime is the primed variant of Astilla, acting as a direct upgrade. Astilla Prime was released alongside Gara Prime and Volnus Prime.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Impact damage.
Fires a single slug every shot instead of individual pellets.
Slugs have a guaranteed Impact proc.
Slugs explode in a 2.4 meter radius after impacting a surface or enemy.

Explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.

Tied with Astilla for the highest accuracy of all shotguns.

Projectiles have travel time.

**Notes:**
As a Signature Weapon, Astilla Prime gains a bonus effect with Gara or Gara Prime, gaining an additional 80 Ammo Maximum, for a total of 200.
Similar to its standard counterpart, because of the weapon's firing mechanics, the Astilla Prime can be practically treated as a mid-ranged assault rifle that uses shotgun mods. But due to the explosive effects of its slugs, the gun is deceptively effective at crowd control.

Weapons
Quickly fire off a volley of deadly bolts with the Attica repeating crossbow.
The Attica is an automatic Tenno crossbow. It has the second-slowest fire rate of all automatic primary weapons but delivers high damage, high critical chance bolts. However, these bolts are also slow, requiring a Tenno to lead their shots.
This weapon can be sold for 7,500.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Puncture damage.

Third highest base damage of all fully automatic rifles, behind Zhuge Prime and Zhuge/Panthera/Panthera Prime.
Tied with Soma/Soma Prime and Baza/Baza Prime for the highest critical multiplier of all fully automatic rifles, and the highest critical multiplier of all bows.
High accuracy.
Pinpoint accuracy when aiming.
Silent.
Projectiles have travel time.
Tied with Panthera Prime for the second slowest fire rate of all fully automatic weapons, after Panthera.

## Basmu
This Sentient war instrument can either barrage targets with explosive bolts or draw on its regenerative battery to create twin plasma beams that chain through targets. When fully drained, Health is leeched from nearby foes for a short period.

### Wiki
Weapons
This Sentient war instrument can either barrage targets with explosive bolts, or, draw on its regenerative battery to create twin plasma beams that chain through targets. When fully drained, Health is leached from nearby foes for a short period.
The Basmu is a Sentient rifle that fires Electricity rounds that create Heat damage explosions on impact. The Alternate Fire channels two beams of Electricity that conducts between targets, with its extreme critical multiplier offset by its low critical chance. When fully expending the battery, the rifle releases multiple energy pulses, staggering enemies and leeching health from them.
Characteristics
This weapon deals Electricity damage, and the explosion deals Heat damage.
Primary fire shoots rounds that explodes 1.7 meter radius upon impact with a surface or enemy.
Initial hit and explosion apply status separately.
Explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
Explosions can benefit from Firestorm (Primed Firestorm) and Cautious Shot.
Alternate Fire shoots two continuous 9 meter beams that lock onto enemies within 30° of the reticle. Each beam can chain up to 5 nearby enemies within 7 meters from the initial target, with each chain dealing 50% of the previous chain's damage.
Heavy Caliber's accuracy penalty on Alternate Fire is minimal.
Fully depleting the battery produces three 10 meter pulses that deals up to 10 True damage, staggers nearby enemies and heals the user's health by 10x the damage done to each enemy per pulse.
This weapon does not use ammo pickups; ammo regenerates over time.
Can use the Basmu-exclusive Dreadful Killshot.
Cannot equip Sinister Reach or Combustion Beam.
Siphoning pulse gives crowd control and healing on emptying the weapon's charge.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 11 | **Riven Disposition:** 4

### Auto
**Damage:** electricity: 39
Crit Chance: 15.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2x | Status Chance: 29.00% | Fire Rate: 12

Crit Chance: 15.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2x | Status Chance: 29.00% | Fire Rate: 12

## Batoten
Hound melee attacks pierce hard with Impact and Puncture Damage.

### Acquisition
The Batoten is automatically acquired upon claiming a Hound configured with the Dorma Model. Note that this weapon also takes up one Sentinel inventory slot.

### Wiki
Hound melee attacks pierce hard with Impact and Puncture damage.
Batoten is a Hound weapon that pierces through enemies with Impact and Puncture damage.
This weapon can be sold for 5,000.
Characteristics
This weapon inflicts Impact and Puncture damage.
Advantages:
Very high critical multiplier.
Good status chance.
Disadvantages:
Low critical chance.
**Notes:**
The Akaten, Batoten and Lacerten are all statistically identical (speed, critical, status, etc.) other than their physical damage types. All three are 50%/50% split between two physical damages, with the three weapons covering the three possible combinations.
Batoten only uses standard melee attacks. It does not allow the Hound to block or perform special attacks, nor does it have its own combo counter.
As such, Batoten cannot equip mods that affect heavy attacks (e.g. Killing Blow), slide attacks (e.g. Maiming Strike), combo counter (e.g. Blood Rush), finishers (e.g. Finishing Touch), or blocking (e.g. Focused Defense). It also cannot equip any Set Mods (e.g. Gladiator Might).
Range mods like Reach do increase the hounds melee range. Their base attack range is very low and because of that they tend to miss many of their attacks. Equipping a range mod makes their attacks hit more consistently.
Heavy attack windup speed (through Melee Elementalist) increases the hounds attack speed.
Actual attack speed mods have no effect.
**Type:** Companion Weapon | **Riven Disposition:** 3

### Normal Attack
**Damage:** impact: 150, puncture: 150
Crit Chance: 10.00% | Crit Multiplier: 3x | Status Chance: 15.00% | Fire Rate: 1

## Baza
Make easy prey of enemies with this quiet, agile, and pinpoint accurate Tenno submachine gun.

### Acquisition
The Baza's blueprint can be researched from the Tenno Lab in the dojo.

### Wiki
Weapons
Make easy prey of enemies with this quiet, agile, and pinpoint accurate Tenno submachine gun.
The Baza is a Tenno silenced submachine gun with a high critical chance, critical multiplier, fire rate and accuracy at the cost medium range damage falloff and a small magazine.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Puncture damage.
This weapon is Silent.
Low zoom when aiming.
Shorter damage falloff distance for a hit-scan weapon.
Very high accuracy per shot, even at full auto.
Negligible recoil.
Silent nature allows for stealth usage.
Lower zoom allows for a more stable view.
Has linear damage falloff from 100% to 50% starting at 22m until 34m target distance (distances are affected by Projectile Speed).
Cannot equip Terminal Velocity.
Poor ammo economy.
Baza Prime
**Notes:**
The weapon's firing sounds increases in pitch when 15 or fewer rounds are remaining in the magazine, indicating that the weapon's magazine is almost empty.
The weapon has minimal zoom when aiming, helpful for mods that add bonuses while aiming like Argon Scope.
**Tips:**
The Baza's high accuracy allows it to land headshots consistently compared to other automatic weapons, which along with its good critical hit chance makes it ideal for builds using Argon Scope.
Its lower status chance compared to some other automatic rifles can be alleviated with the Hunter Munitions mod, allowing the weapon to spread Slash procs rapidly to enemies, which helps the weapon a lot versus heavy armor.
Its silenced nature makes it synergize well with Ivara, as firing the weapon will not interrupt her Prowl ability.
Since the Baza has very high accuracy, it can use Heavy Caliber with almost no drawback and increasing the weapon's overall damage output.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 7 | **Riven Disposition:** 3

### Normal Attack
**Damage:** impact: 5.76, slash: 3.52, puncture: 6.72
Crit Chance: 26.00% | Crit Multiplier: 3x | Status Chance: 10.00% | Fire Rate: 16.67

## Boar
A shotgun with low accuracy and strong recoil, but able to deliver its payload in full-auto. The Boar is best used at close range.

### Wiki
Weapons
A shotgun with low accuracy and strong recoil, but able to deliver its payload in full-auto. The Boar is best used at close range.
The Boar is a Tenno automatic shotgun. Compared to other shotguns, the Boar characteristically bears less accuracy and damage per pellet. With the widest shot cone of all available shotguns, the Boar excels at dealing with large groups of lightly armored enemies at close quarters but quickly loses its effectiveness at mid-long ranges. The gun makes up for its shortcomings with its high rate of fire and larger magazine capacity.
Can be sold for 7,500.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Impact damage.
Innate multishot of 8 pellets.
Large magazine size.
Wide spread is ideal for crowd control.
Lowest base damage of all pellet shotguns.
Has linear damage falloff from 100% to 50% from 15m to 25m target distance (distances are affected by Projectile Speed).
Ammo inefficient.
Strong recoil, although manageable.
Boar Prime
The Boar's blueprint is dropped by Drekar Troopers.
**Notes:**
Incarnon Genesis
Upon completion of The Duviri Paradox and Angels of the Zariman, and unlocking The Steel Path, players can earn Boar Incarnon Genesis from The Circuit's Steel Path to place on their Boar with the help of Cavalero in the Chrysalith. This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.
Alternatively, the Boar Incarnon Genesis can be bought from Cavalero for 120, complete with the resources used in its installation. This purchase can only be made once.

### Wiki
The Boltor is an automatic rifle that rapidly fires heavy bolts that can pin your foes to the walls.
The Boltor is a Tenno assault rifle with the characteristic of firing bolt projectiles instead of conventional bullets. This weapon requires the user to take into account projectile travel time while aiming at targets, as well as the projectile drift, making the Boltor difficult to use effectively beyond medium ranges.
This weapon can be sold for 7,500. This weapon is a weapon crafting ingredient for Boltace.
Kronen + Boltor → Boltace
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Puncture damage.
Bolts fired stick to their target, and will fling bodies on kill.
The body will damage targets in its path.
Corpses can be pinned to terrain.
Flung bodies help in dealing with crowds.
Low recoil.
Projectiles have travel time with slight arcing.

Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Puncture damage.
Bolts fired stick to their target, and will fling bodies on kill.
The body will damage targets in its path.
Corpses can be pinned to terrain.
Flung bodies help in dealing with crowds.
Low recoil.
Projectiles have travel time with slight arcing.
Boltor
Telos Boltor
This weapon can be sold for 7,500.
Vaultings
On February 9th, 2016, it was announced that Boltor Prime would enter the Prime Vault and be retired from the reward tables on February 16, 2016. Any preexisting components or fully-built weapons will remain as is.
Boltor Prime along with Ankyros Prime, Boar Prime, Dakra Prime, Mag Prime, and Rhino Prime were unvaulted on June 27, 2017 to August 1, 2017.
Boltor Prime, along with Ankyros Prime, Hikou Prime, Scindo Prime, Nyx Prime, and Rhino Prime, was once again unvaulted on July 24, 2018 to September 25, 2018. It resurfaced again together with the same line-up from July 16th, 2019 to September 3rd, 2019.
**Notes:**
The lack of Slash damage means that the mods Sawtooth Clip and Fanged Fusillade will have no effect on this weapon.
The innate Vazarin polarity matches Primed Cryo Rounds and Galvanized Aptitude.
Due to its above average status chance, users will often use Rime Rounds instead of Cryo Rounds to make Viral or Magnetic damage, which requires a Madurai polarity.
Incarnon Genesis
Upon completion of The Duviri Paradox and Angels of the Zariman, and unlocking The Steel Path, players can earn Boltor Incarnon Genesis from The Circuit's Steel Path to place on their Boltor with the help of Cavalero in the Chrysalith. This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.

## Braton
The Braton's high rate of fire and accuracy make it a favorite among the Tenno.

### Acquisition
A fully built Braton can be purchased from the Market for 25,000.
It can be sold for 7,500.

### Wiki
The Braton's high rate of fire and accuracy make it a favorite among Tenno.
The Braton is a Tenno assault rifle with balanced damage, fire rate, and accuracy. It is a versatile rifle, performing effectively against all enemies without specialty.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Slash damage by a small margin.
Low recoil.
Mk1-Braton
Braton Prime
Braton Vandal
**Notes:**
Incarnon Genesis
Upon completion of The Duviri Paradox and Angels of the Zariman, and unlocking The Steel Path, players can earn Braton Incarnon Genesis from The Circuit's Steel Path to place on their Braton with the help of Cavalero in the Chrysalith. This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.
Evolution
Braton Incarnon Genesis#Evolutions
**Tips:**
When used extensively or playing solo, the ammunition of an unmodded Braton can burn down quite quickly. Consider switching to your pistol or utilizing melee combat when possible.
When fighting at long ranges, consider firing in short bursts or taking a calculated risk and charging forward. The ammo saved from avoiding sustained long range fighting far and away compensates for any sizable amount of close range damage.
Using Metal Auger and/or Shred makes the Braton useful for taking down large crowds without wasting too much ammo although the mods take up a sizable amount of mod capacity.

## Bubonico
Rain down a triple volley of explosive disease bladders from a distance, then move in for the kill by unloading a multi-shot barrage of toxic barbs with primary fire.

### Acquisition
The Bubonico's blueprint can be researched from the Bio Lab in the dojo.

### Wiki
Weapons
Rain down a triple volley of explosive disease bladders from a distance, then move in for the kill by unloading a multi-shot barrage of toxic barbs with primary fire.
The Bubonico is an Infested Shotgun Arm-Cannon that launches a barrage of Toxin blasts from its primary fire, while its Alternate Fire shoots three explosive Viral shots in a burst. The arm-cannon does not have ammo reserves, and instead regenerates ammo.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Slash damage and innate Toxin on its primary fire and Viral on its Alternate Fire.
Primary fire shoots 7 pellet barrages in a fully automatic fashion.
Alternate fires a three round burst that explode in a 7 meter radius upon impact with a surface or enemy.
Initial hit and explosion apply status separately.
Explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
This weapon does not use ammo pickups; ammo regenerates over time.
Has a 1.5 second delay after the weapon stops firing before regenerating ammo. Then, regenerates 9 ammo per second; takes 3 seconds to regenerate a fully depleted battery excluding the delay. This delay is affected by Reload Speed.
Does not benefit from mods that increase maximum ammo, due to lack of an ammo pool.
Primary Fire:
Innate punch through allows hitting multiple targets in line.
High accuracy.
Alternate Fire:
Pinpoint accuracy.
Primary Fire:
Projectiles have travel time.
Alternate Fire:
Multiplied consumption results in only three bursts before recharging.
Slow projectiles have travel time with heavy arcing.
Explosion inflicts self-stagger.
Explosion has a headshot multiplier of 1x and cannot trigger headshot conditions.
Explosion has linear damage falloff from 100% to 50% from central impact.
Coda Bubonico
**Type:** Shotgun | **Mastery Req:** 13 | **Riven Disposition:** 1

## Burston
The Burston fires 3-round bursts, which provides a balance between the lethality of automatic rifles and the accuracy of semi-automatic rifles.

### Acquisition
The Burston's blueprint and the resources required for its construction are awarded upon completion of Vor's Prize quest. Additional blueprints can be purchased from the Market.
Can be sold for 7,500.

### Wiki
Weapons
The Burston fires 3-round bursts, which provides a balance between the lethality of automatic rifles and the accuracy of semi-automatic rifles.
The Burston is a Tenno burst-fire assault rifle characteristically known for its three-round bursts.
Characteristics
This weapon deals equal physical damages.
Can equip the Burston-exclusive Triple Tap mod (Conclave only).
Burston Prime
**Notes:**
Incarnon Genesis
Upon completion of The Duviri Paradox and Angels of the Zariman, and unlocking The Steel Path, players can earn Burston Incarnon Genesis from The Circuit's Steel Path to place on their Burston with the help of Cavalero in the Chrysalith. This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.
Evolution
Burston Incarnon Genesis#Evolutions
**Tips:**
Be patient and find a rhythm with shooting to fire at a faster rate.
There are various means to counteract the Burston's climbing recoil;
Move the mouse down during each burst. Doing this can allow all three rounds of a burst to be headshots at medium range and can also be used to keep all bullets on target for long-distance shots.
At long distances, aiming the first shot at the torso of enemies will help to guarantee at least one headshot in a burst.
Stabilizer can almost negate the recoil generated, making it easier to keep the bullets on target.
Use Rifle Ammo Mutation to regain extra ammo if needed.
If you can't perform well with this weapon at long ranges, consider equipping the Heavy Caliber mod, as the Burston can do just as well at close range to mid-range combat.
**Type:** Rifle | **Riven Disposition:** 5

### Normal Attack
**Damage:** impact: 10, slash: 10, puncture: 10
Crit Chance: 6.00% | Crit Multiplier: 1.6x | Status Chance: 18.00% | Fire Rate: 5

### Incarnon Form
**Damage:** heat: 3
Crit Chance: 30.00% | Crit Multiplier: 3x | Status Chance: 30.00% | Fire Rate: 20

### Incarnon Form Radial Attack
**Damage:** heat: 3
Crit Chance: 30.00% | Crit Multiplier: 3x | Status Chance: 30.00% | Fire Rate: 20

## Burston Prime
Once thought lost to the ages, attempts to reengineer the Burston Prime never fully replicated this weapon's power and agility.

### Wiki
Once thought lost to the ages, attempts to reengineer the Burston Prime never fully replicated this weapon's power and agility.
The Burston Prime is the Prime variant and direct upgrade of the Burston, sporting higher stats across the board including fire rate, damage, status, and critical chance.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Slash damage.
Can use the Burston Prime-exclusive Gilded Truth.
Burston
Crafting
The Burston Prime can be sold for 7,500.
**Notes:**
Incarnon Genesis
Upon completion of The Duviri Paradox and Angels of the Zariman, and unlocking The Steel Path, players can earn Burston Incarnon Genesis from The Circuit's Steel Path to place on their Burston with the help of Cavalero in the Chrysalith. This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.
Evolution
Burston Incarnon Genesis#Evolutions
**Tips:**
The Burston Prime has a fast burst, making it decent in long-range combat.
The Burston has arguably better ammo efficiency than the Soma or Synapse due to its burst-fire nature.
Fire rate mods (Speed Trigger, Shred, Gilded Truth, etc.) reduce the delay between bursts, but did not increase the fire rate of the actual bullets themselves. As of Update 17.4.3, fire rate mods now increase the fire rate of the burst itself.
It isn't advised to put a Heavy Caliber on the Burston Prime as this weapon benefits more in close to mid range situations with its high accuracy and damage over long range situations where hitting enemies with all bullets becomes more difficult.
Modifying the magazine can sometimes be problematic, as you might be reduced to only 1 or 2 rounds in your last shot. A Max Rank Wildfire or a Rank 3 Magazine Warp will give you a magazine of 54, allowing you 18 bursts before reloading. This ensures you will always fire 3 rounds in every burst.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 12 | **Riven Disposition:** 5

**Damage:** impact: 13.8, slash: 18.4, puncture: 13.8
Crit Chance: 18.00% | Crit Multiplier: 1.8x | Status Chance: 30.00% | Fire Rate: 5

### Incarnon Form
**Damage:** heat: 13
Crit Chance: 28.00% | Crit Multiplier: 3x | Status Chance: 30.00% | Fire Rate: 20

### Incarnon Form Radial Attack
**Damage:** heat: 13
Crit Chance: 28.00% | Crit Multiplier: 3x | Status Chance: 30.00% | Fire Rate: 20

## Buzlok
Mark your targets and unleash a barrage of bullets, the Buzlok's homing rounds always find the enemy. Marked targets are more likely to take critical hits.

### Acquisition
The Buzlok's blueprint can be researched from the Chem Lab in the dojo.
This weapon can be sold for 7,500.

### Wiki
Weapons
Mark your targets and then unleash a barrage of bullets, the Buzlok's homing rounds always find the enemy. Marked Targets are more likely to take critical hits.
The Buzlok is a Grineer slug assault rifle with unique homing capabilities. Its Alternate Fire launches a tracer round that sticks to NPCs or enemies, making all subsequent shots home in on the marked target. Successful homing shots gain a critical chance bonus.
Characteristics
This weapon primarily deals Impact damage on its primary fire.
Alternate Fire shoots a tracer round projectile that makes primary fire shoot homing rounds on the tracked entity, lasting 20 seconds or until target death.
Successfully landing a tracer round grants a 20 second buff that adds 50% absolute critical chance. This buff is retained on target death, and is not affected by mods.
Tracer round mechanic allows for free aiming, and shooting around cover to strike targets.
Once a target is selected with tracking beacon, the weapon will fire on that target until the beacon disappears or the enemy is killed, making it difficult to use against multiple targets or change targets.
Has a unique Z-axis recoil, which creates irregular aimed accuracy after firing a burst.
Projectiles have travel time on main fire and tracer round which may make tagging a weak spot difficult at range.
**Type:** Sniper | **Mastery Req:** 11 | **Riven Disposition:** 5

### Normal Attack
**Damage:** impact: 30, slash: 6, puncture: 24
Crit Chance: 23.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2.5x | Status Chance: 21.00% | Fire Rate: 6.25

### Beacon
**Damage:** puncture: 3
Crit Chance: 0.00% | Crit Multiplier: 1x | Status Chance: 0.00% | Fire Rate: 1.67

## Carmine Penta
Enveloped in deep red composite enamel, this Penta variant features a higher fire-rate and over-sized magazine.

### Wiki
Weapons
Enveloped in deep red composite enamel, this Penta variant features a higher fire-rate and over-sized magazine.
The Carmine Penta is a special variant of the Penta grenade launcher, featuring a higher fire rate and doubled magazine size.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Blast damage.
Can only have five grenades out at once.
Extra projectiles generated by Multishot will count towards the limit of five live grenades.
Grenades explode in 4 meter radius on impact with a surface or enemy.
Initial hit and explosion apply status separately.
Explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
Can benefit from Firestorm (Primed Firestorm) and Cautious Shot.
Explosions can cause self-stagger.
Can use the launcher-exclusive Adhesive Blast mods, as well as the Penta-exclusive Tether Grenades and Napalm Grenades mods.
Pinpoint accuracy.
Heavy Caliber's accuracy penalty is minimal on this weapon.
Grenades have slow travel time and heavy arcing.
Slow reload speed.
Grenades have a high bouncing capability which makes it hard to stack on a desired spot.
Can bounce off teammates or companions, potentially causing self stagger.
This can be countered with the Adhesive Blast mod which will make the grenades stick to any surface.
Tether Grenades counters this, with the additional benefit of keeping a tethered enemy from leaving the blast radius.
Explosion inflicts self-stagger.
Explosion has a headshot multiplier of 1x and cannot trigger headshot conditions.
Explosion has linear damage falloff from 100% to 50% from central impact.
Penta
Secura Penta
This weapon's blueprints and components can be obtained from Pluto Proxima or Veil Proxima Corpus-controlled Abandoned Derelict Caches in Empyrean missions. All parts can be traded between players.

## Cedo
Launch a glaive that ricochets off surfaces in bursts of elemental damage, then perforate enemies with primary fire's precision buck-shot. Damage increases with each Status Effect afflicting a target. Lavos will transmute a small portion of any ammo pickup to Primary Ammo when wielding his signature Cedo.

### Wiki
Launch a glaive that ricochets off surfaces in bursts of elemental damage, then perforate enemies with primary fire's precision buck-shot. Damage increases with each Status Effect afflicting a target. Lavos will transmute a small portion of any ammo pickup to Primary Ammo when wielding his signature Cedo.
Bekran Zaft says this of Execution Day.
The jailers gathered in the auditorium. With a slow funereal tread, Lavos escorted Javi to the execution dais. He gently raised a hood over Javi's head, cobra-like. He turned, then, to face the Orokin Warden, shotgun trembling in his mighty hands.
The silence, Zaft says, was absolute.
But then from beneath the hood came a whisper. Javi had some final words for his student.
The Orokin jailer shifted uncomfortably, looked to his functionaries. Would the execution even take place?
Lavos gave a stiff bow to Javi... and activated his power.
Javi's skin peeled off in one grisly sloughing. He liquefied into a biological soup.
A cheer went up from the assembled jailers.
Lavos gathered the remains of Javi tenderly in his hands. A soft glow emanated from them. As, in one horrible moment, Javi's Orokin oppressors realized what they beheld. And then: panic. Lavos was transmuting the remains.
A twining, living snake seethed up Lavos's left arm. Javi, transformed, and still - after a fashion - alive, just as they had feared. A second snake coiled around his right arm, this one sprouting from Lavos's own flesh.
Then Lavos leapt into the midst of the assembly, hurling vials left and right, bathing the hapless screaming jailers and functionaries - and their Warden - in icy vitriol. Not one survived - not for long, anyway.
And Bekran Zaft? Lavos bowed to her and moved on.

## Cedo Prime
A golden shotgun, forged for those who deliver judgment.

### Wiki
Weapon
A golden shotgun, forged for those who deliver judgment.
Cedo Prime is the Primed counterpart of the Cedo alchemical shotgun, acting as a direct upgrade. Cedo Prime was released alongside Lavos Prime and Dual Zoren Prime.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Puncture damage.
Deals +60% bonus damage per unique status effect on target.
On the Primary Fire, this bonus is additive to all base damage bonuses, such as Point Blank and Galvanized Savvy.
On direct hits from the Alternate Fire glaive, this bonus is additive with Galvanized Savvy, and multiplicative to normal base damage bonuses like Point Blank. See Condition Overload (Mechanic) for more info.
On explosions from the Alternate Fire glaive, this bonus does not apply.
Primary Fire shoots wide-spread, close-ranged buckshot in a fully automatic fashion.
Innate 0.8 meter punch through.
Innate multishot of 7 pellets.
Alternate Fire launches a glaive that explodes in a 6 meter radius on impact with a surface or enemy and bounces up to 11 times.
Direct hit and explosion apply status separately.
Direct hit deals Slash damage and has the listed status chance of 50%, affected by status chance bonuses and modded elements.
Explosion deals Blast damage and applies two separate guaranteed procs. The number of procs cannot be increased with status chance bonuses.
The first proc can be any element modded onto the weapon, or the innate Blast damage. The chance for each element follows normal status weighting rules.
The second proc has an even chance to be any of the following; Heat, Cold, Electricity, or Toxin.
Explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
Consumes no ammo.
Multishot affects the number of glaives released per use, each with their own trajectory.
Innate Shotgun Ammo Mutation effect when used by Lavos.
Innate two Madurai and one Vazarin polarities.
**Type:** Shotgun | **Mastery Req:** 15 | **Riven Disposition:** 1

## Cernos
Evoking the design of ancient bows, the high impact Cernos is perfect for hunting down highly shielded enemies.

### Acquisition
The Cernos's blueprint can be purchased from the Market.

### Wiki
Weapons
Evoking the design of ancient bows, the high impact Cernos is perfect for hunting down highly shielded enemies.
The Cernos is a Tenno Bow, and the first one available to new players that deals mainly Impact damage over another type.
This weapon can be sold for 10,500. It is also a weapon crafting ingredient for Mutalist Cernos.
Cernos → Mutalist Cernos
Mutalist Cernos → Proboscis Cernos (extra Cernos is needed if player wants to keep one copy of Mutalist Cernos)
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Impact damage.
Cernos Prime
Rakta Cernos
**Tips:**
Despite what its description says, the Cernos is most effective against Grineer, which are primarily armored, not shielded. Its damage spread being weighted primarily towards Impact means the majority of the weapon's damage will inherit the element's Faction Damage Bonus against them, dealing solid damage even through their armor.
Impact's status effect of reducing the target's Mercy threshold also logically counteracts their armor, allowing heavy enemies to be executed even with most of their health remaining, once their Overguard is broken, after suffering enough Impact procs.
While the Cernos has a low base status chance relative to its rate of fire and projectile count, this can easily be fixed with modding.
**Type:** Bow | **Mastery Req:** 6 | **Riven Disposition:** 4

### Uncharged Shot
**Damage:** impact: 171, slash: 9.5, puncture: 9.5
Crit Chance: 36.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2x | Status Chance: 18.00% | Fire Rate: 1

**Damage:** impact: 342, slash: 19, puncture: 19
Crit Chance: 36.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2x | Status Chance: 18.00% | Fire Rate: 1

## Cernos Prime
A noble bow to hunt hellish beasts.

### Acquisition
Unlike most Prime gear, Cernos Prime is permanently unvaulted, with relics being obtainable through Abandoned Derelict Caches in Corpus Railjack missions.
Vaultings
On September 11th, 2018, it was announced that Cernos Prime, along with Valkyr Prime and Venka Prime, would enter the Prime Vault and be retired from the reward tables on September 25th, 2018. Any preexisting components or fully-built weapons will remain as is.

### Wiki
A noble bow to hunt hellish beasts.
The Cernos Prime is the Prime variant of the Cernos. Aside from increased damage, the Cernos Prime is unique in that it fires three arrows per shot in a horizontal spread, and has an Alternate Fire mode that shoots vertically, and deals bonus damage on headshots. It was released alongside Valkyr Prime and Venka Prime.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Impact damage.
Deals 50% bonus damage on headshots.
Charged shots have innate 1 meter punch through.
Innate multishot of 3 arrows.
Alternate Fire can toggle a horizontal or vertical spread shot.
On kill, bodies will follow the arrow that killed them, damaging enemies in their path and pinning the corpse to walls.
Can use the bow-exclusive Thunderbolt mod.
Fire rate mods apply double their bonuses.
Innate two Madurai polarities.
Fourth highest base damage of all bows, behind Daikyu, Lenz and Kuva Bramma.
Pinpoint accuracy when aiming (RMB).
Silent.
Very wide spread.

## Cinta
The versatile Cinta can fire arrows in quick succession, quickly charge a shock wave, or fully charge a powerful focused shot.

### Wiki
Weapons
The versatile Cinta can fire arrows in quick succession, quickly charge a shock wave, or fully charge a powerful focused shot.
Cinta is a Duviri bow wielded by the Dax Arcus. It is capable of firing "perfect" shots when releasing the trigger just before a full charge, resulting in a large projectile for dealing with groups of enemies.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Puncture damage.
In addition to uncharged shots, this weapon can fire one of two types of charged shots, depending on where the charge meter is released.
Releasing the shot within 70% to 90% of a full charge, indicated by white brackets and the reticle turning yellow, will perform a "Perfect Shot", firing an energy shockwave dealing pure Heat damage, guaranteed Knockdown, and a significantly larger projectile with Infinite Body Punch Through.
Releasing the shot when the meter is fully charged will perform a "Charged Shot", firing a single arrow dealing heavy Puncture damage, with higher critical chance and critical multiplier than that of Perfect Shots.
Cinta's main and component blueprints are acquired by solving Enigma puzzles in Duviri, having a 30% chance to drop one part from any puzzle completion. All blueprints are tradable.
**Notes:**
There is a gap between the Perfect Shot window and full charge where releasing the charge will fire uncharged shots instead.
Although similar to Nataruk, Cinta can make use of Split Flights, whereas the former cannot.
Known Bugs
Galvanized Aptitude is multiplicative to base damage sources on uncharged, perfect and charged shots.
**Trivia:**
Cinta is the Malay/Indonesian word for "love", and Spanish for "tape" or "ribbon".
Prior to Update 33.5, the Cinta had an Alternate Fire that could prime landmines while having the player leap backward. This feature was only intended for the Dax Arcus enemy, and was removed from the player version due to animation oddities and low damage.
**Type:** Bow | **Mastery Req:** 6 | **Riven Disposition:** 4

### Quick Shot
**Damage:** slash: 90, puncture: 360
Crit Chance: 20.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2x | Status Chance: 30.00% | Fire Rate: 0.667

### Charged Shot
**Damage:** slash: 160, puncture: 640
Crit Chance: 36.00% | Crit Multiplier: 3x | Status Chance: 32.00% | Fire Rate: 0.667

### Perfect Shot
**Damage:** heat: 600
Crit Chance: 20.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2x | Status Chance: 34.00% | Fire Rate: 0.667

|---|---|---|
| Blueprint | 1 | — |

## Coda Bassocyst
Amplify shockwaves of devastating sound with this Technocyte virus infested shotgun that hungers for blood, and desires nothing more than to feed upon its enemies. Alternative fire at enemies not vulnerable to Mercy Kills to swarm them with infested mites, while firing at vulnerable ones will perform a ranged execution.

### Wiki
Weapons
Amplify shockwaves of devastating sound with this Technocyte Coda infested shotgun that hungers for blood, and desires nothing more than to feed upon its enemies. Alternative fire at enemies not vulnerable to Mercy Kills to swarm them with infested mites, while firing at vulnerable ones will perform a ranged execution.
Coda Bassocyst is a unique Coda boombox shotgun that shoots wide sonic projectiles. Its Alternate Fire launches an Infested mite that latches onto enemy heads to inflict Impact and Magnetic status effects, while an enemy opened to a Parazon Mercy Finisher will perform a teleporting execution that strengthens damage and multishot.
Characteristics
This weapon primarily deals Blast damage.
Performing a Mercy Kill grants +100% damage and Multishot for 20 seconds.
Primary Fire:
Innate Infinite Body Punch Through.
Projectiles have a thickness of ? meters.
Alternate Fire:
Launches a mite that latches up to 6 enemies, highlighting their heads for 5 seconds while dealing Impact and Magnetic damage and status effects per second for 10 seconds.
Consumes 4 ammo per shot.
Does not benefit from Multishot, neither in terms of total damage nor the number of hit enemies.
Has a large auto-aim cone (~90 degrees).
Does not trigger headshot conditions and does not benefit from headshot multipliers.
If the target is opened to a Mercy, the user will instead teleport to them and automatically perform a Mercy Finisher.
Bonuses stack additively with respective mods.
Buff duration is refreshed on Mercy kills.
Mercy Finisher needs a target under the teeth reticle, so to avoid unwanted teleports aiming above or below a target is a workaround.
Cannot be used without a target that is within 25 meters.
Projectile Speed mods like Fatal Acceleration, Galvanized Acceleration will extend this range.
Innate Madurai polarity.
Adversary Weapon:
Comes with an additional bonus Impact, Heat, Cold, Electricity, Toxin, Magnetic, or Radiation damage, which increases the listed base dam

## Coda Bubonico
With increased magazine size, the Coda Bubonico was born of the Technocyte virus' need to spread infection. Primary Fire rate ramps up with continuous fire.

### Wiki
Weapons
With increased magazine size, the Coda Bubonico was born of the Technocyte virus' need to spread infection. Primary Fire rate ramps up with continuous fire.
Coda Bubonico is the Coda variant of the Infested Bubonico arm-cannon, sporting increased critical chance, status chance, fire rate, and increased magazine size, but possess a brief spool-up to achieve optimal fire rate.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Slash damage and innate Toxin on its primary fire and Viral on its Alternate Fire.
Primary fire shoots 7 pellet barrages in a fully automatic fashion.
Alternate fires a three round burst that explode in a 7 meter radius upon impact with a surface or enemy.
Initial hit and explosion apply status separately.
Explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
This weapon does not use ammo pickups; ammo regenerates over time.
Has a 1.5 second delay after the weapon stops firing before regenerating ammo. Then, regenerates 9 ammo per second; takes 4 seconds to regenerate a fully depleted battery excluding the delay. This delay is affected by Reload Speed.
Does not benefit from mods that increase maximum ammo, due to lack of an ammo pool.
Innate two Naramon and one Madurai polarities.
Adversary Weapon:
Comes with an additional bonus Impact, Heat, Cold, Electricity, Toxin, Magnetic, or Radiation damage, which increases the listed base damage of the weapon by 25%-60%.
Valence Fusion can optionally change the resulting output damage type, and can upgrade the bonus increase (up to 60%).
Polarizing the weapon increases its max rank by 2, capping at rank 40 after 5 polarizations, granting the weapon additional mod capacity.
Elemental Vice can be applied to the weapon after it has reached rank 40, allowing the bonus damage type to be swapped freely.
Unlike Kuva and Tenet weapons directly acquired from a Kuva Lich or Sisters of Parvos, the listed Mastery Rank requirement is enforced.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 17 | **Riven Disposition:** 1

## Corinth
Deliver a massive punch with each blast of this shotgun, or launch a round that explodes mid-air knocking down nearby enemies.

### Wiki
Weapons
Deliver a massive punch with each blast of this shotgun, or launch a round that explodes mid-air knocking down nearby enemies.
The Corinth is a large Tenno designed shotgun, featuring two firing modes. The primary fire is a powerful buckshot supported by incredible critical stats, while the Alternate Fire launches a grenade projectile that creates an 9.4-meter explosion after traveling for 20 meters in midair possessing high status chance.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Puncture damage on its primary fire and Blast damage on its alt-fire.
Primary Fire shoots a wide-spread, close-ranged buckshot:
Innate multishot of 6 pellets.
Alternate Fire launches a grenade that creates a 9.4 meter area of effect explosion after traveling 20 meters in midair (distance is affected by Projectile Speed).
Physical contact with the grenade has a guaranteed Impact proc.
The 20 meter minimum arming distance mitigates self-stagger.
Explosion does not need direct line of sight to deal damage and will penetrate walls.
Reloads one shell at a time at a rate of 0.22 seconds per shell, which can be interrupted at any point, allowing for planned reloading.
Innate two Madurai polarities.
Primary Fire:
Tied with Corinth Prime for the highest critical chance and critical multiplier of all shotguns.
Tied with Hek and Vaykor Hek for the narrowest pellet spread of all shotguns.
**Type:** Shotgun | **Mastery Req:** 8 | **Riven Disposition:** 4

## Corinth Prime
Heavy in the hand with a bone-crunching kickback. This Prime version of this shotgun adds remote detonation of the weapon's airburst round.

### Wiki
Prime
Heavy in the hand with a bone-crushing kickback. This Prime version of this shotgun adds remote detonation of the weapon's airburst round.
The Corinth Prime is the primed variant of the Corinth shotgun with improved status chance, magazine size, buckshot fire rate, and air burst damage and explosion radius.
The air burst no longer has a set range before exploding allowing it to damage enemies from farther away, but in turn no longer has a delayed fuse and must be manually detonated with Alternate Fire.
Instead of individual rounds, the weapon reloads the entire magazine at once with a slower reload speed.
Corinth Prime was released alongside Titania Prime and Pangolin Prime.

## Daikyu Prime
Long believed lost, the mighty Daikyu Prime was the pride of many a Tenno dojo. Designed to strike the foe where they are weakest, its powerful shots repay careful marksmanship.

### Wiki
Prime
Long believed lost, the mighty Daikyu Prime was the pride of many a Tenno Dojo. The strength demanded to draw it is repaid in added range and power.
Daikyu Prime is the Primed counterpart of the Daikyu heavy bow, with enhancements to its already high damage, Critical Chance, Critical Multiplier, and Status Chance. It also does not need to be fully charged to fire, and has increased critical chance against weak points. Daikyu Prime was released alongside Yareli Prime and Kompressa Prime.
Characteristics
This weapon primarily deals Puncture damage.
Can be fired after charging for at least 25% of maximum charge, but with reduced damage.
Forced Impact status effect on both charge levels.
Can fully benefit of Internal Bleeding.
Shots have an innate 3 meter punch through, the highest of all bows.
Weak point hits have +250% Critical Chance bonus.
Bonus stacks additively with mods like Point Strike and Primary Acuity.
On kill, bodies will follow the arrow that killed them, damaging anyone in their path and pinning the corpse to walls.
Medium-to-high zoom level when aiming.
Can equip the bow-exclusive Thunderbolt mod, and the Daikyu-exclusive Spring-Loaded Broadhead and Amalgam Daikyu Target Acquired mods.
Fire rate mods apply double their bonuses.
Innate two Madurai polarities.
Daikyu
**Tips:**
This weapon is perfect for Internal Bleeding: with the guaranteed Impact proc and slow fire rate, each arrow has 70% chance to apply bleed.
With Internal Bleeding + Vile Acceleration + Critical Delay, the weapon has guaranteed crit (120%), a fast charge time (0.42s), and is still just above the threshold to have 70% proc rate from Internal Bleeding.
Due to the high headshot multiplier obtainable via Amalgam Daikyu Target Acquired, and the quirk of Gas status effects being able to headshot, it's a very strong pairing to run Gas on this weapon, as the Gas clouds can take full advantage of Target Acquired.

Deconstructor Prime only uses standard melee attacks. It does not allow the Sentinel to block or perform special attacks, nor does it have its own combo counter.
As such, Deconstructor Prime cannot equip mods that affect heavy attacks (e.g. Killing Blow), slide attacks (e.g. Maiming Strike), combo counter (e.g. Blood Rush), finishers (e.g. Finishing Touch), or blocking (e.g. Focused Defense). It also cannot equip any Set Mods (e.g. Gladiator Might).

The projectiles may possibly obstruct view when firing if the energy color is a bright color such as yellow, blue, or green.
The projectiles ricochet off of a Nullifier Crewman's bubble.
Due to the projectiles firing from alternating barrels, this has a convergence effect where the bolts will meet at the point the crosshair is on. This will result in the bolts crossing in an 'X' if they miss their target.
Incarnon Genesis
Upon completion of The Duviri Paradox and Angels of the Zariman, and unlocking The Steel Path, players can earn Dera Incarnon Genesis from The Circuit's Steel Path to place on their Dera with the help of Cavalero in the Chrysalith. This Incarnon Genesis form grants it evolutions to a transformation and newfound strength.

**Trivia:**
The Dex Sybaris is the third Dex weapon released, after the Dex Furis and the Dex Dakra. It is also the first Dex primary weapon to be released.
The Dex Sybaris has three energy dots near the trigger which indicates that the weapon was released on the third anniversary. The same can be seen for the other Dex weapons: Dex Dakra has two dots, and Dex Furis has one.
Details on this weapon were leaked via the Warframe Companion mobile app.
When firing the Dex Sybaris, the Lotus logo will appear as a muzzle flash, and its color can be customized via the Dex Sybaris's energy color.
The Dex Sybaris is one of the very few guns in-game with a visible full-length bore.
The Dex Sybaris was first released from March 22, 2016 to March 29, 2016 on PC, and from April 9, 2016 to April 15, 2016 for consoles. It was released again from March 24, 2017 to March 27, 2017.

## Drakgoon
The Drakgoon flak cannon sends volleys of intensely hot shrapnel ricocheting around the room. Can be fired in wide or concentrated bursts.

### Acquisition
The Drakgoon's blueprint can be purchased from the Market. Alternatively, it can be acquired from completing the Neptune to Pluto Junction.

### Wiki
Weapons
The Drakgoon Flak Cannon sends volleys of intensely hot shrapnel ricocheting around the room. Can be fired in wide or concentrated bursts.
The Drakgoon is a Grineer flak cannon with a distinct charge mechanic. The longer the weapon is charged, the tighter the spread of the pellets fired, and charged shots gain punch through depending on how long the charge is held, giving the Drakgoon extreme versatility at any range. Additionally, the flak from the Drakgoon can bounce off surfaces once, allowing for a volley to hit more than once if positioned correctly.
This weapon can be sold for 7,500; it is also a weapon crafting ingredient for the Zarr.
Drakgoon → Zarr
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Slash damage.
Shots ricochet once.
This allows one to fire at a wall and kill an enemy behind cover, albeit with some difficulty.
Charged shots have innate 2 meter punch through.
Innate multishot of 10 pellets.
Effective over multiple ranges due to charge/spread mechanic:
Uncharged shots fires wide-dispersal shots to soften up multiple enemies at mid/long range, or quickly hit a single target at short range.
Charged shots fires a tightly focused blast over longer ranges for high single-target damage.
Can use the Drakgoon-exclusive Fomorian Accelerant mod.
High charge speed.
No damage falloff.
Shrapnel projectiles have travel time.
Shrapnel projectiles slow down by a great margin after ricocheting.
Shrapnel will vanish after 2 seconds of leaving the weapon.
Kuva Drakgoon
**Notes:**
The projectiles bounce once when not charged, and bounce twice when fully charged. 
The "Flak" from the weapon works with Mag's Magnetize and can headshot if it enters the field at a high enough point.
**Type:** Shotgun | **Mastery Req:** 5 | **Riven Disposition:** 5

## Dread
Dread is the calling card of The Stalker. It fires arrows that can decapitate.

### Wiki
Weapons
Dread is the calling card of the Stalker. It fires arrows that can decapitate.
Dread is a deadly hunting bow that fires bladed arrows, serving as the Stalker's signature weapon. This bow sports a large amount of Slash damage, the highest critical chance stat matched only by the Lenz and Kuva Chakkhurr, and high status chance, causing many shots to inflict Slash procs.
This weapon can be sold for 7,500.
Characteristics
This weapon deals primarily Slash damage.
Can use the Dread-exclusive Unseen Dread mod.
Dread's blueprint is dropped by the Stalker. It is also included in the What Stalker? bundle for 725 in the market.
**Notes:**
Dread has a hidden mechanic similar to Sniper Combo which is not mentioned in game: After landing 2 consecutive hits (including from multishot and punch through) on enemies within 5 seconds, all subsequent shots will deal 2x total damage. After 90 consecutive hits, this is increased to 3x total damage, which is the cap. This effect resets upon missing every arrow (from multishot) in a single shot, or waiting more than 5 seconds between shots.
The Incarnon mode does not interact with the combo mechanic whatsoever. It does not benefit from the damage multiplier, and the current combo count will be frozen until switching back to the normal firing mode; hitting enemies will not increase the combo count, and missing shots or waiting more than 5 seconds between shots will not reset the current combo count.
All "charge weapons" (guns) are capable of holding their charge during a sprint. As long as the user starts the charge, they are able to finish the charge while they are still sprinting. This grants more mobility for the user.
**Type:** Bow | **Mastery Req:** 5 | **Riven Disposition:** 4

Crit Chance: 50.00% | Crit Multiplier: 2x | Status Chance: 20.00% | Fire Rate: 1

## Enkaus
Turn the enemy's worst nightmare into reality with Follie's signature rifle. The Enkaus will instantly kill and dissolve inked enemies that are damaged at low health. Shooting inked enemies siphons ink back into the Enkaus, allowing for a constant flow of creative destruction.

### Wiki
Tenno
Turn the enemy's worst nightmare into reality with Follie's signature rifle. The Enkaus will instantly kill and dissolve inked enemies that are damaged at low health. Shooting inked enemies siphons ink back into the Enkaus, allowing for a constant flow of creative destruction.
Enkaus is Follie's signature beam rifle, hosing down its targets with a stream of Corrosive ink. Its Alternate Fire launches a glob of ink that stains enemies, allowing follow-up primary fire to refund Enkaus ammo and instantly dissolving them when they hit a certain health threshold.
Characteristics
This weapon primarily deals Corrosive damage.
This weapon does not use ammo pickups.
Alternate Fire launches an ink projectile that explodes in an 8 meter radius on contact with enemies or a surface. Enemies hit by the blast are covered with ink for 20 seconds. The projectile will also leave a puddle with a 8-meter radius, lasting for 10 seconds and staining enemies that come into contact with it.
When wielded by Follie, the Alternate Fire applies Inkblot, Follie's passive.
Enemies afflicted by Inkblot are considered ink-stained targets, even if the Enkaus's alternate fire wasn't used.
Shooting ink-stained enemies with the primary fire draws ink back into the weapon, replenishing ammo.
Firestorm increases the area of the paint left on the ground.
Ink-stained enemies will be dissolved and instantly killed if Enkaus drops their health below 35%. Enemies with special states such as Thrax Centurion or Kuva Larvling will not be dissolved to avoid breaking their mechanics. Bosses cannot be instant killed.
When wielded by Garuda, while the enemy is inflicted with Seeking Talons, the insta-kill damage element will change from Corrosive to Slash.
Consumes 15 ammo per shot.
Innate Madurai polarity.
Primary Fire:
Limited range of 30 meters.
Alternate Fire:
Projectile has travel time with heavy arcing.
Explosion has linear Damage Falloff from 100% to 50% from central impact.
**Type:** Rifle | **Mastery Req:** 12
